requirement: Understanding the organization and its context
question: Has the organization determined external and internal issues relevant to OH&S management outcomes (legal, cultural, social, economic, governance, structure, resources, workplace culture)?
evidenceExample: Context analysis, SWOT, Management review inputs

requirement: Understanding the needs and expectations of workers and interested parties
question: Has the organization identified workers and other interested parties and determined their needs and expectations relevant to the OH&S management system?

requirement: Determining the scope of the OH&S management system
question: Has the organization determined the scope, boundaries, and applicability of the OH&S management system and maintained it as documented information?
Has the organization established, implemented, maintained and continually improved an OH&S management system including required processes and their interactions?
Does top management demonstrate leadership and commitment to preventing work-related injury and ill health, ensuring safe workplaces, and integrating OH&S into business processes?
evidenceExample: Management review, Resource allocation, Communication records

question: Has top management established and maintained an OH&S policy including commitments to safe working conditions, elimination of hazards, compliance, continual improvement, and worker consultation?
Have roles, responsibilities, and authorities been assigned and communicated to ensure OH&S management system conformity and performance reporting?
Has the organization established and maintained processes for consultation and participation of workers at all levels, with mechanisms, training, and removal of barriers?
Does the organization determine risks and opportunities to prevent undesired effects and support continual improvement of the OH&S system?

requirement: Hazard identification and assessment of risks and opportunities
question: Has the organization established ongoing processes for hazard identification (work organization, human factors, equipment, non-routine activities, emergencies, incidents, changes) and systematic assessment of OH&S risks and opportunities?

requirement: Determination of legal and other requirements
question: Has the organization identified, accessed, and maintained applicable legal and other OH&S requirements and determined how they apply and are communicated?
Does the organization plan actions to address hazards, risks, opportunities, and compliance obligations, including hierarchy of controls and evaluation of effectiveness?
Has the organization established measurable OH&S objectives at relevant levels, with responsibilities, resources, timelines, and monitoring methods?
Does the organization determine and provide adequate human, infrastructure, technological, and financial resources for the OH&S system?
How does the organization determine competence needs, ensure workers are competent, provide training, evaluate effectiveness, and retain documented evidence?
Does the organization ensure workers are aware of the OH&S policy, their contribution, implications of nonconformance, hazards and risks, and their rights (e.g. removal from imminent danger)?
Has the organization developed processes for internal and external OH&S communications (what, when, with whom, how) with evidence retained?
Does the organization maintain and control documented information required by ISO 45001 (creation, updating, approval, distribution, access, retention, protection)?
Has the organization established criteria and controls for operational processes, adapted work to workers, and managed multi-employer sites and outsourced processes?

requirement: Eliminating hazards and reducing OH&S risks
question: Does the organization apply the hierarchy of controls (elimination, substitution, engineering, administrative, PPE)?
Has the organization established processes to plan and control temporary and permanent changes affecting OH&S performance?
Does the organization control procurement of products, services, contractors, and outsourced processes to ensure conformity with OH&S requirements?
Has the organization developed, implemented, and maintained processes for emergency preparedness including planning, training, testing, evaluation, and periodic revision?
Does the organization determine what to monitor and measure (legal compliance, hazards, objectives, controls), methods, criteria, timing, and evaluation?
Has the organization established processes to evaluate compliance with legal and other OH&S requirements and retain documented evidence?
Does the organization conduct internal audits at planned intervals to verify OH&S system conformity and effective implementation?
Does top management review the OH&S system at planned intervals considering performance, context changes, and improvement opportunities?
Does the organization identify and implement actions to improve OH&S outcomes and enhance system suitability and effectiveness?
Has the organization established processes for reporting, investigating, and managing incidents and nonconformities, and for taking corrective action to prevent recurrence?
evidenceExample: Incident reports, NCR, Corrective action records

question: Does the organization continually improve the OH&S management system by enhancing performance, promoting worker participation, and fostering safety culture?
